title: Safeguard Custom Platform Script
description: JSON Schema for Safeguard Custom Platform scripts. It is intentionally descriptive and permissive so editors can offer autocomplete, hover help, and validation without blocking valid edge-case scripts.

$schema.description: Optional JSON Schema URI for editor support. This can safely be included in uploaded scripts; SPP ignores unknown top-level properties.

Id.description: Unique platform identifier used by Safeguard. Most existing samples use Id.

Name.description: Human-friendly platform name. HTTP-style examples often use Name instead of Id.

BackEnd.description: Execution backend for custom platform scripts. Safeguard custom platform scripts always use Scriptable.

Transport.description: Optional transport hint for the script. This is useful for documentation and editor autocomplete.

Meta.description: Optional free-form metadata for humans, such as version, notes, prerequisites, or warnings.

Imports.description: Optional list of shared import libraries used by the script.

CheckSystem.description: Optional operation that validates connectivity and service account access.

CheckPassword.description: Optional operation that verifies the current managed account password.

ChangePassword.description: Optional operation that rotates the managed account password.

CheckSshKey.description: Optional operation that verifies whether an SSH key is configured.

ChangeSshKey.description: Optional operation that adds, updates, or removes SSH keys.

DiscoverAccounts.description: Optional discovery operation that emits discovered accounts.

DiscoverAttributes.description: Optional discovery operation that emits or gathers asset or account attributes.

DiscoverServices.description: Optional discovery operation that emits discovered services.

CheckFile.description: Optional operation that validates a file-based credential or artifact.

ChangeFile.description: Optional operation that updates a file-based credential or artifact.

CheckSystem_Elevate.description: Optional JIT-style elevate operation using the CheckSystem_Elevate naming convention.

CheckSystem_Demote.description: Optional JIT-style demote operation using the CheckSystem_Demote naming convention.

CheckSystem_EnableAccount.description: Optional JIT-style enable-account operation using the CheckSystem_EnableAccount naming convention.

CheckSystem_DisableAccount.description: Optional JIT-style disable-account operation using the CheckSystem_DisableAccount naming convention.

DiscoverSshHostKey.description: Optional legacy operation that discovers SSH host keys during asset onboarding.

RetrieveSshHostKey.description: Optional legacy operation that retrieves SSH host key details.

DiscoverAuthorizedKeys.description: Optional legacy operation that discovers authorized SSH keys.

RemoveAuthorizedKey.description: Optional legacy operation that removes an authorized SSH key.

EnableAccount.description: Optional legacy JIT operation that enables an account.

DisableAccount.description: Optional legacy JIT operation that disables an account.

ElevateAccount.description: Optional legacy JIT operation that elevates an account.

DemoteAccount.description: Optional legacy JIT operation that demotes an account.

UpdateDependentSystem.description: Optional operation that updates a dependent system after credential rotation.

CheckApiKey.description: Optional operation that validates an API key.

ChangeApiKey.description: Optional operation that rotates an API key.

parameterType.description: Parameter scalar type. The schema accepts common casing variants used by existing samples.

parameterDefinition.description: Describes a single parameter including its type, whether it is required, and an optional default value.

Required.description: Whether Safeguard must supply a value before the operation can run.

DefaultValue.description: Optional default value used when no explicit value is provided.

Description.description: Optional human-readable description for administrators and editor hovers.

parameterObject.description: A single-key object where the property name is the parameter name and the value describes that parameter.

parameterArray.description: Ordered list of parameter declarations for an operation or legacy function definition.

customParameter.description: A custom parameter definition that can be surfaced in the Safeguard UI for per-asset configuration.

Name.description: Internal parameter name referenced by the script.

DisplayName.description: Friendly name shown in the UI or documentation.

Description.description: Short help text explaining what the custom parameter is used for.

Required.description: Whether the custom parameter must be supplied.

DefaultValue.description: Optional default value for the custom parameter.

Sensitive.description: Whether the value should be treated as sensitive by tools and UIs.

customParameters.description: Optional list of reusable custom parameters for the platform.

functionParameters.description: Function parameters can be declared either as a simple string array or in the older parameter-object form used by existing samples.

functionDefinition.description: Reusable function body. Functions can be authored in keyed-object form for autocomplete or in legacy array form for compatibility.

Do.description: Ordered list of function commands in classic scripts.

Commands.description: Ordered list of function commands in CommandType-style scripts.

legacyFunctionDefinition.description: Legacy function definition used by the repository's existing sample scripts.

Name.description: Function name used by Function or Call commands.

functionsSection.description: Top-level function collection. The schema supports both keyed-object functions and the legacy array-of-functions format used by existing samples.

operationDefinition.description: An operation entry point. Declare Parameters, then provide either Do or Commands with the steps to execute.

Do.description: Classic command list used by most existing scripts.

Commands.description: CommandType-style command list used by newer HTTP-oriented examples.

knownCommandPayload.description: Many commands accept additional transport-specific fields such as ResultVariable, Timeout, Url, Headers, Then, Else, Cases, or nested Do blocks. This schema intentionally stays permissive at the command payload level.

knownNestedCommandObject.description: Single-key command object used by classic Do blocks.

HttpSetup.description: Configure shared HTTP request settings such as base address, headers, or sessions.

HttpCookie.description: Read, set, or manage HTTP cookies.

HttpForm.description: Extract, populate, or submit form data.

HttpJsonBody.description: Construct or parse JSON request or response bodies.

HttpUrlEncode.description: URL-encode text for use in query strings or form submissions.

HttpBase64.description: Encode or decode Base64 content for HTTP workflows.

SshSend.description: Send text or commands over an SSH session.

SshExpect.description: Wait for expected SSH output or prompts.

SshExecuteCommand.description: Run a non-interactive command over SSH and capture the result.

SshHostKey.description: Discover or validate SSH host key material.

Condition.description: Branch execution using an If expression with Then and optional Else blocks.

Switch.description: Run one of several blocks based on a match value.

ForEach.description: Iterate over each item in a collection.

While.description: Repeat a block while a condition remains true.

Try.description: Run a block with error handling and optional Catch behavior.

Catch.description: Catch block payload used by error handling flows.

Return.description: Exit the current operation or function and optionally return a value.

Break.description: Stop the current loop or exit early.

Throw.description: Raise an error and stop execution unless it is caught.

Rethrow.description: Re-raise the current error inside a catch flow.

SetItem.description: Create or update a variable in script state.

Declare.description: Alias for SetItem used to declare a variable.

Comment.description: Add an inline note that does not affect runtime behavior.

Delay.description: Pause script execution for a short period of time.

Log.description: Write a diagnostic message to the task log.

Trace.description: Emit detailed trace information for troubleshooting.

Status.description: Report a progress update or user-facing status message.

WriteResponseObject.description: Write a structured response object back to the platform task engine.

WriteResponseProperty.description: Write a single response property back to the platform task engine.

ExecuteDependentCommand.description: Invoke a dependency-related command exposed to the script.

Request.description: Execute an HTTP request and capture the response object.

NewHttpRequest.description: Create a named HTTP request object for later configuration.

BaseAddress.description: Set the base URL that relative request paths resolve against.

Headers.description: Add or update HTTP headers on a request object.

HttpAuth.description: Apply HTTP authentication such as Basic, Bearer, or NTLM.

GetCookie.description: Read a cookie value from the current HTTP session state.

SetCookie.description: Create or overwrite a cookie value for later requests.

ClearCookie.description: Remove a cookie from the current HTTP session state.

GetFormValue.description: Read a field value from an extracted HTML form object.

SetFormValue.description: Populate a field in a form object before submission.

ExtractFormData.description: Parse HTML response content into a reusable form object.

ExtractJsonObject.description: Parse JSON content into an object the script can inspect.

UrlEncode.description: Encode text for safe use in URLs or form payloads.

CryptMd5.description: Generate an MD5 hash value from input text.

CompareShadowHash.description: Compare a password against a salted /etc/shadow hash entry.

ComparePasswordHash.description: Compare a password against a salted hash (batch-mode variant).

CompareMacOsPasswordHash.description: Compare a password against a macOS directory-service hash.

CompareUnixPasswordHash.description: Compare a password against a general Unix password hash.

Connect.description: Open an SSH or Telnet session and create a connection object.

Disconnect.description: Close an existing connection object and end the session.

Send.description: Send interactive text or commands to a terminal session.

Receive.description: Read terminal output into a buffer variable.

ExecuteCommand.description: Run a remote command in batch mode and capture stdout, stderr, and exit status.

DiscoverSshHostKey.description: Retrieve SSH host key data for asset trust workflows.

For.description: Repeat a block a fixed number of times.

Eval.description: Evaluate an expression for side effects or object manipulation.

Split.description: Split text into an array using a delimiter or pattern.

Wait.description: Pause execution for a defined interval.

WriteDiscoveredService.description: Emit a discovered service record from a discovery script.

WriteDiscoveredSshKey.description: Emit a discovered SSH key record.

knownFlatCommandObject.description: Flat command object used by CommandType-style scripts, especially in Commands arrays.

CommandType.description: Command identifier used by the flat command shape.

genericCommandObject.description: Fallback for valid commands that are not yet explicitly modeled in this schema, including older Connect, Send, Receive, Request, and similar samples.

command.description: A command inside Do or Commands. Known command names get autocomplete and hover help, while the schema still permits custom or legacy command shapes.

commandArray.description: Ordered list of commands executed by an operation or function.
